news.title: The president of the Argentine Football Association breaks his silence: do not be fooled by "conspiracy theories"
news.lede: Tapia admits that Spain deserved the 2026 World Cup title. The 2026 World Cup final won't be forgotten in a hurry. Not just for the joy that swept across Spain after the coronation, but for the wave of controversy and the hundreds of conspiracy theories that erupted in Argentina after Ferran Torres's goal stripped the "tango dancers" of their world crown. Explanations for the defeat have spread like wildfire in Argentina over the past hours.

news.title: 'Replay the final': More than 88,000 Argentina fans call for replay after bitter World Cup final defeat
news.lede: Spain's dramatic 1-0 extra-time victory over Argentina in the 2026 FIFA World Cup final may have settled the destination of the trophy, but the controversy surrounding the showpiece continues to gather momentum days after the final whistle. Football News: After Argentina's defeat in the World Cup final, over 88,000 fans have signed a petition calling for a replay, citing controversial refereeing decisions. The campaign highlights alleged misconduct by referee Slavko Vinčić, leading to accusations of corruption and demanding an investigation by FIFA.

news.title: Numeris: 77% of Canada saw FIFA men's World Cup; average viewer watched 15 hours
news.lede: The 2026 FIFA men's World Cup was the most-watched edition of the international soccer tournament in Canadian history, according to an audience measurement organization. Numeris says that 77 per cent of Canada watched the tournament at some point this summer, up from 71 per cent in 2018 and 68 per cent in 2022. The number of hours watched was also up over the previous two tournaments in Russia and Qatar, with the average viewer taking in 15 hours of matches. Canada's men's national team was eliminated from the tournament with a 3-0 loss to Morocco on July 4 in the Round of 16.

news.title: How the World Cup was really watched: Inside the data behind football's biggest live blogs
news.lede: From Curaçao's historic goal to Spain's final glory, analysis of 1,362 World Cup live blogs reveals what kept 24 publishers' audiences hooked (it wasn't just the scoreline). I watched it a lot when I was a kid, but found myself losing interest as I became an adult and increasingly unwilling to waste an afternoon watching a 1-0 game. In time I replaced watching TV with a live blog, typically booting up the Sky Sports News app on my phone or typing the fixture into a Google search bar. It became a much more convenient way to see the scoreline at a glance, the run of play and which players were having a blinder (or a game to forget).

news.title: 5 Weeks, 48 Teams, One Summer to Remember – The 2026 World Cup Through My Eyes
news.lede: The 2026 World Cup has come to an end, with Spain becoming champions. Prior to the start of the tournament, I set out to write my first, first-person piece on my memories and experiences throughout the World Cup. I felt that after the 2026 World Cup, it would be a fantastic opportunity to do a follow-up and bring up some more memories and experiences that I’ve experienced across this summer’s edition. As I mentioned in the previous piece, the World Cup isn’t just four weeks- sorry, this time, five weeks of football.

news.title: From Popp to Diani: 10 women’s transfers you may have missed during the men’s World Cup
news.lede: Sam Kerr’s and Alexia Putellas’s moves were hard to miss but these transfers may have escaped your attention. There were a host of big women’s football transfers which were hard to miss across June and July, despite the dominance of the men’s World Cup. Among them were Beth Mead’s move from Arsenal to Manchester City, Mary Earps and Georgia Stanway returning to the Women’s Super League with London City Lionesses and Arsenal, Sam Kerr leaving Chelsea to return to Gotham FC and, most prominently, Alexia Putellas’s game-changing switch from Barcelona to London City. But here are 10 transfers you might have missed while attention was focused on the action in the US, Canada and Mexico.

news.title: FIFA launch Argentina investigation after World Cup final brawl
news.lede: This content is provided by Exco Player and may use cookies or similar technologies. Please click 'Allow and Continue' below to load the content. FIFA has launched an investigation into potential disciplinary breaches by Argentina following their World Cup final defeat to Spain. Following the final whistle in East Rutherford, New Jersey, Argentina midfielder Leandro Paredes appeared to throw punches at members of the Spain squad as they celebrated their victory on the pitch.

news.title: Bayern Munich star Michael Olise plays in NYC pickup game
news.lede: You have to give it to Bayern Munich and France superstar Michael Olise, the man does love football. Well, after France lost the third-place game at the World Cup to England, the 24-year-old could not stay away from the game for long, so he joined a pickup game in New York City. Imagine you are stretching trying to get loose after sitting in a cube all day pouring through spreadsheets and you look across the field and have to defend Olise. Not just Olise, though, but Olise wearing some wild pants.
